title: bus.bead.lifecycle v1
description: Composite lifecycle stream for beads — every bead-relevant transition flows here keyed by `event_type`. Producer is hearth-loom (internal/bus/publisher.go); consumers include deer-flow Forge (which dispatches by event_type). Bench results have a richer payload and live on a sibling channel `bus.bead.bench_completed.v1` rather than here. The 15 enumerated event_types cover both internal worker lifecycle (dispatched, container_died, startup_reset, rescued_wip, pickup_analysis_complete, ac_self_verified, scope_divergence, bead_closed, bead_failed) and external Forge-facing PR/CI events (pr_opened, pr_merged, checks_passed, lint_passed, tests_passed, falsified). Payload is event_type-specific; sub-schemas are enumerated under properties.payload.oneOf for the Forge-facing events. Producer event_types may attach free-form payloads.

bead_id.description: Cross-project bead id (e.g. nervous-bus-immg, loom-78wk). Pattern is permissive to allow legacy ids.

task_id.description: hearth-loom task/dispatch id correlating this event to a specific shuttle run (e.g. 'af899084996493906'). Distinct from bead_id because a single bead may be retried under multiple task_ids.

event_type.description: Discriminator for which transition this event represents. Consumers dispatch on this field.

ts.description: RFC3339 UTC timestamp of when the transition occurred.

attempt_id.description: Per-dispatch attempt key correlating this event to a specific execution attempt. Empty for pre-dispatch events.

correlation_id.description: Optional correlation key for tracing cross-service request chains (e.g. bus.beads.command.v1 request_id).

actor.description: Who triggered this transition: poller, dispatcher, loomie, autoloom, human, command-bus.

project.description: Absolute project path for the bead (e.g. /home/user/projects/hearth-loom/main).

payload.description: Event-specific data. For Forge-facing event_types (pr_opened, pr_merged, checks_passed, lint_passed, tests_passed, falsified) the structure is documented via the anyOf sub-schemas below; for producer-internal lifecycle event_types the payload is free-form (the open object case at the end of anyOf accepts anything). anyOf rather than oneOf is intentional: a well-formed pr_opened payload satisfies BOTH the pr_opened branch and the free-form branch, and we don't want to reject it for being too well-formed.

linter.description: Linter identifier (e.g. ruff, eslint, golangci-lint, clippy).

framework.description: Test framework (e.g. pytest, go-test, vitest, cargo-test).

cause_of_death.description: Short tag for why the bead is being abandoned (e.g. 'ci_failed_3x', 'merge_conflict', 'human_reject').

anyOf.description: Free-form fallback for producer-internal event_types (dispatched, container_died, startup_reset, rescued_wip, pickup_analysis_complete, ac_self_verified, scope_divergence, bead_closed, bead_failed). No constraints — producer may add fields without a schema bump.
